# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/http/server.ts";
import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v12.0.0/router.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
  createdAt: Date;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const router = new Router();

// Helper function to construct dynamic keys
function constructKey(prefix: string[], ...args: (string | number)[]): string[] {
  return [prefix.join(":"), ...args.map(arg => arg.toString())];
}

// Helper function to validate and sanitize input
function sanitizeInput(input: string): string {
  return input.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, "");
}

// Helper function to validate email
function isValidEmail(email: string): boolean {
  const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
  return emailRegex.test(email);
}

// Search endpoint with flexible filters
router.get("/search", async (ctx) => {
  try {
    const { 
      name, 
      email, 
      minAge, 
      maxAge, 
      sortBy = "createdAt", 
      sortOrder = "desc",
      limit = "100"
    } = ctx.request.url.searchParams;

    const filters: Record<string, any> = {};
    
    if (name) filters.name = sanitizeInput(name);
    if (email) {
      if (!isValidEmail(email)) {
        ctx.response.status = 400;
        ctx.response.body = { error: "Invalid email format" };
        return;
      }
      filters.email = email;
    }
    if (minAge) filters.minAge = parseInt(minAge);
    if (maxAge) filters.maxAge = parseInt(maxAge);

    const limitNum = Math.min(parseInt(limit), 1000);
    
    // Build dynamic key for efficient lookup
    const baseKey = ["users"];
    const searchKey = [...baseKey];
    
    if (filters.name) {
      searchKey.push("name", filters.name);
    }
    if (filters.email) {
      searchKey.push("email", filters.email);
    }
    
    const results: User[] = [];
    const iterator = kv.list<User>({ prefix: searchKey });
    
    for await (const { key, value } of iterator) {
      // Apply additional filters
      let matches = true;
      
      if (filters.minAge && value.age < filters.minAge) matches = false;
      if (filters.maxAge && value.age > filters.maxAge) matches = false;
      
      if (matches) {
        results.push(value);
      }
      
      if (results.length >= limitNum) break;
    }
    
    // Sort results
    results.sort((a, b) => {
      if (sortOrder === "desc") {
        return b[sortBy as keyof User] > a[sortBy as keyof User] ? 1 : -1;
      }
      return a[sortBy as keyof User] > b[sortBy as keyof User] ? 1 : -1;
    });
    
    ctx.response.body = results;
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

// Create user endpoint
router.post("/users", async (ctx) => {
  try {
    const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
    
    const { id, name, email, age } = body;
    
    if (!id || !name || !email || age === undefined) {
      ctx.response.status = 400;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "Missing required fields" };
      return;
    }
    
    if (!isValidEmail(email)) {
      ctx.response.status = 400;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "Invalid email format" };
      return;
    }
    
    const user: User = {
      id,
      name: sanitizeInput(name),
      email,
      age,
      createdAt: new Date()
    };
    
    // Store user with multiple indexed keys for efficient search
    const userKey = ["users", id];
    const nameKey = ["users", "name", sanitizeInput(name), id];
    const emailKey = ["users", "email", email, id];
    const ageKey = ["users", "age", age.toString(), id];
    
    await kv.atomic()
      .set(userKey, user)
      .set(nameKey, user)
      .set(emailKey, user)
      .set(ageKey, user)
      .commit();
    
    ctx.response.status = 201;
    ctx.response.body = user;
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

// Get user by ID
router.get("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  try {
    const { id } = ctx.params;
    
    const key = ["users", id];
    const result = await kv.get<User>(key);
    
    if (!result.value) {
      ctx.response.status = 404;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
      return;
    }
    
    ctx.response.body = result.value;
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

// Update user
router.put("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  try {
    const { id } = ctx.params;
    const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
    
    const key = ["users", id];
    const result = await kv.get<User>(key);
    
    if (!result.value) {
      ctx.response.status = 404;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
      return;
    }
    
    const updatedUser: User = {
      ...result.value,
      ...body,
      id
    };
    
    // Update with new indexed keys
    await kv.atomic()
      .set(key, updatedUser)
      .set(["users", "name", sanitizeInput(updatedUser.name), id], updatedUser)
      .set(["users", "email", updatedUser.email, id], updatedUser)
      .set(["users", "age", updatedUser.age.toString(), id], updatedUser)
      .commit();
    
    ctx.response.body = updatedUser;
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

// Delete user
router.delete("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  try {
    const { id } = ctx.params;
    
    const key = ["users", id];
    const result = await kv.get<User>(key);
    
    if (!result.value) {
      ctx.response.status = 404;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
      return;
    }
    
    // Delete all indexed keys
    await kv.atomic()
      .delete(key)
      .delete(["users", "name", result.value.name, id])
      .delete(["users", "email", result.value.email, id])
      .delete(["users", "age", result.value.age.toString(), id])
      .commit();
    
    ctx.response.body = { message: "User deleted successfully" };
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Internal server error" };
  }
});

// Health check endpoint
router.get("/health", (ctx) => {
  ctx.response.body = { status: "OK", timestamp: new Date().toISOString() };
});

// Security middleware
const securityMiddleware = async (ctx: any, next: any) => {
  // Rate limiting (basic implementation)
  const clientIP = ctx.request.ip;
  const rateLimitKey = ["rate_limit", clientIP];
  
  const rateLimitResult = await kv.get<{ count: number; resetTime: number }>(rateLimitKey);
  const now = Date.now();
  
  if (rateLimitResult.value && rateLimitResult.value.resetTime > now) {
    if (rateLimitResult.value.count > 100) {
      ctx.response.status = 429;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "Rate limit exceeded" };
      return;
    }
  }
  
  // Update rate limit
  const newCount = rateLimitResult.value ? rateLimitResult.value.count + 1 : 1;
  const resetTime = now + 60000; // 1 minute
  
  await kv.set(rateLimitKey, { count: newCount, resetTime });
  
  await next();
};

// Apply middleware
const app = new Deno.serve({
  port: 8000,
  handler: securityMiddleware,
});

app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

console.log("Server running on http://localhost:8000");
